So what kind of business you know a lot to get all the basic obvious questions Let's talk about the less obvious questions here like number one. Okay is like what is the Lifetime value of a customer When so in disguise if you're in if you're selling digital marketing here, there's there's two Factors that go into play at once here. And this is this is like sort of a holy grail for selling what you sell here. Okay There's the intersection of lifetime value of a customer and cash flow That's your those your two your your two levers here that you have to satisfy Meaning that let's just say if someone advertises and spends $10,000 a week on facebook, right? And let's say from that 10,000 Let's say they spend 10k. What's making a 10k a week? That's their their budget for facebook, right and from that 10k a week They get back 6k a week As they do it in other words, so the facebook charges you every day if you're advertising to hit your credit card when you get to a Certain portion to a certain number and then only if you're huge can you get a credit line? But for most people they have to pay when they get to like a thousand or 1500 whatever the facebook decides is the credit Limbo they charge you so they don't charge you up front for ads They charge you after the ads are placed, but they don't let it get too far So the point is you're basically paying into a day So for ads that run on a monday You have to pay for them on a monday and let's say The 60 let's say this so this 10,000 dollars gets paid every day And let's say so the average would be what 50 will make it 1500 a week. Let's say no Let's make about 8 8 50 they get back 8 50 a day Let's say what they're selling is credit cards Like they're able to charge it on your advertising who gets the money back by credit card right away, right? So if they're getting the money back by credit card, remember there might be a three day hold So if you're on strike for instance when I get my money on strike, I might make 30 grand that day But I don't get it for three days So I have to lay that money out. That's that's a cash flow issue Okay, now so imagine if someone has to front 10,000 a week and they get back this 6,000 they theoretically lose 4,000 this week But on top of that they also had to wait like another week to really get to 6,000 Because it takes time for it to catch up until you get the credit card money back and so forth, right? Or let's say even worse Let's say that this facebook ads was a two call system that they filled out a lead form yet They called them back and you got this 60 dollars back But it was even another week later two weeks later till this it's based on the sales cycle When do you close the sales that the facebook ads create? What if part of what you're doing is a seven day free trial like I do it? I don't get my money for seven. I give over in seven days. I know my process great I said give them seven days So it's fine. If someone doesn't want to do it. That's their loss, but I don't get my money for seven days That's a cash flow issue That's a cash flow issue, right? Every bit remember you must if you're selling to business owners You got to think like a business owner and talk like a business owner because if you're not bringing these things up here in your presentation and using it to overcome that if you don't understand What your business owner is going through you are never going to be a fucking great closer you have to step into your customer's shoes and Experience what they're going through and speak to them from that perspective You write your straight line. This is it guys. You write your straight line presentation Based on from what your customer is experiencing not what you want in your world of disneyland I know it'll be great and and this is what I said I had this great comment I made at a frank current event many many years ago And I said, you know some of you guys you guys are great digital walkers But you think that if you if you master the art of digital marketing, you don't have to learn to speak to another human being How the fuck you're gonna make money if you can't explain what you do so Now i'm focusing on digital marketing right now, but this goes to everything Whatever it is you're selling one of the strengths I have as a salesperson is because I have a lot of business experience I am able to put myself in my Prospects shoes And say what do they need to hear? To understand that i'm right I'm right. They might be in a business where the product they're selling they have to import it from china And carry free and this is very important guys for all of you in every business What's the cash flow model of the person that you're trying to close? Are they in a business where they have negative cash flow or positive cash? If they're in a business with a negative cash flow Then you they're going to be very price sensitive Because if you're selling them a service that's going to have to lay out more money Right that's not going to work for you. So you want to essentially design your process with something that that actually helps improve their cash flow So let's get down now. Let's get one. That's how you do it one more question Me So yesterday I was Had a young kid over the house here of South African and he was here Friend of a friend of mine, right and he's in network marketing. He does very very well very successful, right? And he asked me this question about, you know, what do you think a network? They said well, that's like, you know, what do I think of uh of sales? Or what do I think of computers? I mean, you know, there's good ones and there's bad ones Right. So network marketing, you know, how good or bad it is is directly related to what the fuck you're selling is a network market Here's when network marketing sucks network marketing sucks when when you have a a tangible product when they're selling a product like uh Something that's that that has a cost of goods sold where because of the inherent inefficiencies in network marketing of distribution like lots of people all this inefficient distributing, right? Versus you're never going to have to sell me. It's just it's not true that having 15 100 000 distributors Is going to be as economic as having one truck drop at a warehouse and going to stores where everyone shows up Well amazon's model that's going to be more efficient In terms of distribution course. So what you see very often with network marketing companies that have certain types of products Is they're overpriced? They might be very good products, but they're not cheap So any network marketing company is trying to sell their stuff that's saying oh our stuff is cheaper than the competition Is probably full of shit now there are Some circumstances for instance when you're dealing with things like travel and unused capacity Which is why certain things like the travel and hotel industry Lend themselves to things like barter and network marketing because they have hotels that are sitting vacant There's no cost to that So there's a lot of value that a network marketing company can provide to us to hoteliers and airlines if there's empty seats Because they're filling unused capacity So the and it's not even delivered because the people are going to the destination So i'm just gonna be one example here. So it's about the product If you're like i'll give you an example if it's like this if it was like this I won't even say name if it's some certain type of energy drink We have to ship the bottles, okay You know Very often that energy drink will be a lot more expensive than a comfortable drink of the same quality because of the inefficiency of distributing all the bottles If the product is great And it really works You have a flying chance But just beware of those types of situations The other part is ask yourself this In a network marketing deal How much of the product is being actually sold to end users? Versus being stored in the garages of people in the down line If it's all about people signing up other people and then people buying a minimum amount of products and get their commission check That's not that's a scam But with the legitimate network marketing company where people are actually just like avon like i mean they sell this at a Tupperware where they sell this that they're pulling legitimate So there's great network marketing companies and they shade you ones like anything else in the world Can you make a lot of money in network marketing apps are fucking lootily you can it's got some great advantages and some disadvantages The answer is be very careful Choose the network marketing company that you get involved very carefully and also Who's the person that's above you who's down line? Are you in make sure you're being supported by the people that bring you in You can do really well if you're careful with what product you're selling and you have good support from above That's the best answer I can give so one more question.
